Most AI tools generate text.

AI agents complete tasks.

Connect tools, automate workflows, and get real work done.

Considering the process we're in, I don't think it's something that's escaping; even in the best scenario where the S&P 500 maintains its momentum, I assume we'll experience a process like this. However, for me, long-term buying will be at 6,100 and below after this process.

BREAKING: IBM just admitted your AI agent forgets everything the moment it finishes a task.

> Every mistake. Repeated.
> Every inefficiency. Repeated.
> Every failure. Repeated.

They built the fix.

> Every AI agent starts each task from zero:
> No memory of what worked.
>

> Tell me why and how [business] can achieve [goal]
> AI will 100% of times say why and how
> Human can analyze and say “it can’t”

That’s why you need a self-verification loop with AI.

Karpathy's autoresearch project just went viral. Everyone's asking 'is this cool?'

I'm asking: how do I pipe it into an n8n workflow by end of day.

Answer: 3 nodes.

→ HTTP Request node hits the autoresearch API
→ Code node extracts the summary
→ Notion/Slack node delivers
